title: microcos henrici subsp. henrici
intro: Microcos henrici subsp. henrici is a plant species commonly known as the Malaysian Oak or Borneo Oak, belonging to the family Malvaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and rheumatism, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Microcos henrici subsp. henrici are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and optimal preparation methods.
